title: All Butter Pie Crust (way better)
ingredientLines: 2 and 1/2 cups (313g) all-purpose flour, plus more as needed (spooned & leveled)
1 cup (16 Tbsp; 226g) unsalted butter, chilled and cubed
1/2 cup (120ml) ice water, plus more as needed
directionLines: 1. Mix the flour, sugar, and salt together in a large bowl. AddĀthe cubed butter on top.

2. Using a pastry cutter, food processor, or two forks (pastry cutter is ideal, see post above), cut the butter intoĀthe dry ingredients until all flour is coated. YouŌĆÖre looking for pea-sized bits of flour coated butter. A few larger bits of butter is OK.

3. Measure 1/2 cup (120ml) of water in a cup. Add ice. Stir it around. From that, measure 1/2 cup (120ml) of water since the ice has melted a bit. Drizzle the cold water in, 2 Tablespoons (30ml) at a time, and stir after each addition. Stop adding water when the dough comes together easily and begins to form large clumps. The dough will feel moist and a little sticky, but not feel overly wet. Do not add any more water than you need to. I always use aboutĀ1/2 cup (120ml) of ice water.

4. Place pie dough on a lightly floured work surface. Using flouredĀhands, fold the dough into itself until the flour is fully incorporated into the fats.ĀForm it into a ball.ĀDivide dough in half. Using your hands, flatten each half into a 1-inch thick disc.
5. Wrap each disc tightly in plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2Āhours and up to 5 days before using in a pie recipe.

6. When rolling out the chilled pie doughĀdiscs, use gentle force with your rolling pin. Start from the center of the discĀand work your way out in all directions, turning the dough with your hands between rolls. Smooth out the edges if you notice cracks. (See video above.) Keeping your work surface, rolling pin, and hands lightly floured makes rolling out easier.
7. Proceed with the pie per your recipeŌĆÖs instructions.
